In traditional medicine, the tree's sap is also believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating wounds and preventing infection. The sap of the manchineel tree has also been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. Aconitum officinale, also known as Monk's Hood or Wolf's Bane, is a perennial plant in the Aconitum genus, commonly known as monk's hood or wolf's bane. The health benefits of Aconitum officinale include its use as a pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aconitum officinale include alkaloids such as aconitine, mesaconitine, and hypaconitine, as well as other compounds like aconitigenin and aconitidine. The most common herbal preparations made with Aconitum officinale include tinctures, infusions, and ointments, which are often used in traditional Chinese medicine and herbalism.
